oils/regex.test.sh::Operator chars ; & b.reason: the [[ parser still rejects operator tokens like ; and & after =~ earlier than bash, so the conditional parse diagnostics and status flow diverge

oils/regex.test.sh::Fatal error inside [.reason: arithmetic expansion errors inside [[ =~ ]] now return status 1, but stderr text still differs from bash

oils/regex.test.sh::Malformed regex.reason: malformed regex conditionals still surface parser errors from mvdan/sh rather than bash's conditional-expression diagnostics

oils/regex.test.sh::Unquoted { is a rege.reason: gbash does not emit the stderr diagnostic that bash outputs for invalid regex in [[ =~ ]]

oils/regex.test.sh::Regex with == and no.reason: the [[ parser still reports generic operator parsing errors instead of bash's regex-aware conditional diagnostics in this == case

oils/regex.test.sh::Parse error with 2 w.reason: the [[ parser still reports a generic invalid operator error instead of bash's conditional-expression syntax diagnostics when =~ is followed by two words

oils/regex.test.sh::unquoted [a b] as p.reason: the [[ parser still tokenizes some bracket-expression forms differently from bash, causing an early operator parse error

oils/regex.test.sh::unquoted_[a__b]_as_p.reason: the [[ parser still tokenizes some bracket-expression forms differently from bash, causing an early operator parse error
